title:
Lesson 1:
Following & Waiting
description: '¿Me acompañas?' = Will you come with me? 'Sígueme' = Follow me. 'Espérame' = Wait for me. These are common phrases for coordinating movement with someone.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Will you come with me to the market?

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Follow me, I know a shortcut.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Wait for me, I need to grab my keys.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Okay, I'll wait for you here.

title:
Lesson 2:
Hurry Up / Take It Easy
description: 'Apúrate' = Hurry up. 'Con calma' = Take it easy / calmly. These opposites are very common in everyday speech.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Take it easy, there's still time.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.spanish: No, el autobús sale en cinco minutos.
dialogue.english: No, the bus leaves in five minutes.

title:
Lesson 3:
A Moment Please
description: 'Permíteme un momento' = Excuse me a moment (neutral). 'Permítame' = more formal version. 'Dame un momento' = Give me a moment. 'Haz algo' = Do something.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.spanish: Permíteme un momento, tengo una llamada.
dialogue.english: Excuse me a moment, I have a call.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.spanish: Dame un momento más, por favor.
dialogue.english: Give me one more moment, please.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: Está bien. Mientras tanto, haz algo útil.
dialogue.english: That's fine. In the meantime, do something useful.
